html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty — Part One

The Twelve Minor Prophets

THE TWELVE — TREI ASAR

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls Scroll of the Twelve Prophets

Hoshea — Chapters 1 through 14

Take a Wife of Whoredom — I Will Betroth You Forever — Sow Righteousness — My Heart Recoils Within Me — Return O Yisra'EL

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran — The Scroll of the Twelve

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

HOSHEA — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 14 — THE COVENANT MARRIAGE AND THE COVENANT RETURN

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 1 The Word of YHWH to Hoshea — Take a Wife of Whoredom — Yizre'el — Lo-Ruhamah — Lo-Ammi — Yet the Number of Yisra'EL Shall Be as the Sand

1 The word of YHWH that came to Hoshea the son of Be'eri, in the days of Uzziyahu, Yotam, Akhaz, and Khizkiyahu, kings of Yehudah, and in the days of Yarov'am the son of Yo'ash, king of Yisra'EL.

2 When YHWH first spoke through Hoshea, YHWH said to Hoshea, Go, take to yourself a wife of whoredom and have children of whoredom, for the land commits great whoredom by forsaking YHWH.

3 So he went and took Gomer, the daughter of Divlayim, and she conceived and bore him a son.

4 And YHWH said to him, Call his name Yizre'el, for in just a little while I will punish the house of Yehu for the blood of Yizre'el, and I will put an end to the kingdom of the house of Yisra'EL.

5 And on that day I will break the bow of Yisra'EL in the valley of Yizre'el.

6 She conceived again and bore a daughter. And YHWH said to him, Call her name Lo-Ruhamah, for I will no more have compassion on the house of Yisra'EL, to forgive them at all.

7 But I will have compassion on the house of Yehudah, and I will save them by YHWH their Creator. I will not save them by bow or by sword or by war or by horses or by horsemen.

8 When she had weaned Lo-Ruhamah, she conceived and bore a son.

9 And YHWH said, Call his name Lo-Ammi, for you are not my people, and I am not your Creator.

10 Yet the number of the children of Yisra'EL shall be like the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured or numbered. And in the place where it was said to them, You are not my people, it shall be said to them, Children of the living Creator.

11 And the children of Yehudah and the children of Yisra'EL shall be gathered together, and they shall appoint for themselves one head. And they shall go up from the land, for great shall be the day of Yizre'el.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves the Twelve Minor Prophets as a single scroll — the earliest known Hebrew text of the complete Twelve. Hoshea opens the scroll as the first of the Trei Asar. The marriage command of Chapter 1 is the covenant sign-act that structures the entire book: Hoshea's marriage to Gomer enacts the covenant relationship between YHWH and Yisra'EL. The three children's covenant names — Yizre'el, Lo-Ruhamah, Lo-Ammi — are the covenant indictment of the northern kingdom in three covenantal stages.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Hoshea Chapter 1 in full. Verse 10 — yet the number of the children of Yisra'EL shall be like the sand of the sea — is the covenant reversal of the Lo-Ammi judgment: in the same breath that announces covenant rejection, YHWH announces covenant restoration. The covenant name You are not my people will be reversed to Children of the living Creator.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. The three covenant name-children form the covenant structure of the book: Yizre'el — covenant judgment of Yehu's dynasty — Lo-Ruhamah — covenant withdrawal of compassion — Lo-Ammi — covenant rupture of the covenant relationship itself. Each is progressively more severe. The reversal oracle of verses 10-11 promises the precise inverse of all three.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — take to yourself a wife of whoredom. The covenant command that has generated more interpretive debate than almost any other prophetic command: whether Gomer was already a prostitute when Hoshea married her, or whether she became one afterward, the covenant point is identical — the marriage that began in covenant fidelity became the covenant portrait of YHWH's marriage to Yisra'EL. The covenant prophet does not merely speak about covenant infidelity — he lives it.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 2 Plead With Your Mother — She Said I Will Go After My Lovers — I Will Allure Her Into the Wilderness — I Will Betroth You to Me Forever

1 Say to your brothers, Ammi, and to your sisters, Ruhamah.

2 Plead with your mother, plead — for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband — that she put away her whoring from her face, and her adultery from between her breasts,

3 Lest I strip her naked and make her as in the day she was born, and make her like a wilderness, and make her like a parched land, and kill her with thirst.

4 Upon her children also I will have no compassion, because they are children of whoredom.

5 For their mother has played the whore. She who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.

6 Therefore I will hedge up her way with thorns, and I will build a wall against her, so that she cannot find her paths.

7 She shall pursue her lovers but not overtake them, and she shall seek them but shall not find them. Then she shall say, I will go and return to my first husband, for it was better for me then than now.

8 And she did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the wine, and the oil, and who lavished on her silver and gold, which they used for Ba'al.

9 Therefore I will take back my grain in its time, and my wine in its season, and I will take away my wool and my flax, which were to cover her nakedness.

10 Now I will uncover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers, and no one shall rescue her out of my hand.

11 And I will put an end to all her mirth, her feasts, her new moons, her Shabbatot, and all her appointed feasts.

12 And I will lay waste her vines and her fig trees, of which she said, These are my wages, which my lovers have given me. I will make them a forest, and the beasts of the field shall devour them.

13 And I will punish her for the feast days of the Ba'alim when she burned offerings to them and adorned herself with her ring and jewelry, and went after her lovers and forgot me, declares YHWH.

14 Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak tenderly to her.

15 And there I will give her her vineyards and make the Valley of Akhor a door of hope. And there she shall answer as in the days of her youth, as at the time when she came out of the land of Egypt.

16 And in that day, declares YHWH, you will call me My Husband, and no longer will you call me My Ba'al.

17 For I will remove the names of the Ba'alim from her mouth, and they shall be remembered by name no more.

18 And I will make for them a covenant on that day with the beasts of the field, the birds of the heavens, and the creeping things of the ground. And I will abolish the bow, the sword, and war from the land, and I will make you lie down in safety.

19 And I will betroth you to me forever. I will betroth you to me in righteousness and in justice, in steadfast love and in mercy.

20 I will betroth you to me in faithfulness. And you shall know YHWH.

21 And in that day I will answer, declares YHWH. I will answer the heavens, and they shall answer the earth,

22 And the earth shall answer the grain, the wine, and the oil, and they shall answer Yizre'el.

23 And I will sow her for myself in the land. And I will have mercy on Lo-Ruhamah, and I will say to Lo-Ammi, You are my people. And he shall say, You are my Creator.

24 Say to your brothers, Ammi, and to your sisters, Ruhamah.

25 For their mother has played the whore. She who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant lawsuit and covenant re-wooing of Chapter 2 moves from covenant indictment — verses 2-13 — through covenant allurement — verse 14 — to covenant betrothal — verses 19-20. The Valley of Akhor — the place of covenant failure in Yehoshua 7 — becomes the covenant door of hope. YHWH redeems the covenant geography of past failure.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verses 19-20 — I will betroth you to me forever, in righteousness and justice, in steadfast love and mercy, in faithfulness — and you shall know YHWH. The covenant betrothal formula is the most comprehensive covenant declaration in the prophetic corpus: six covenant attributes form the bride-price of the renewed covenant marriage.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 16 — you will call me My Husband — Ishi — and no longer My Ba'al. The covenant renaming: Ba'al means husband or master and was used both as a divine name for the Canaanite deity and as a common term for husband. YHWH insists on Ishi — my man — stripping the covenant vocabulary of every association with the competing covenant system.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — she did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the wine, and the oil, and who lavished on her silver and gold, which they used for Ba'al. The covenant irony at the center of the whoredom accusation: the resources used to worship Ba'al were given by YHWH. The covenant community attributed YHWH's covenant provision to the Ba'al system and redirected the covenant gifts back into the competing covenant worship. The covenant gifts became covenant weapons against the covenant Giver.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 3 Go Again, Love a Woman — Bought Her for Fifteen Shekels of Silver — Many Days Without King or Prince — Afterward They Shall Return

1 And YHWH said to me, Go again, love a woman who is loved by another man and is an adulteress, even as YHWH loves the children of Yisra'EL, though they turn to other gods and love cakes of raisins.

2 So I bought her for fifteen shekels of silver and a homer and a lethech of barley.

3 And I said to her, You must dwell as mine for many days. You shall not play the whore, or belong to another man. So will I also be to you.

4 For the children of Yisra'EL shall dwell many days without king or prince, without sacrifice or pillar, without ephod or household gods.

5 Afterward the children of Yisra'EL shall return and seek YHWH their Creator, and Dawid their king, and they shall come in fear to YHWH and to his goodness in the latter days.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The second marriage command — Go again, love — is the covenant re-enactment after the covenant rupture of Chapter 2. Hoshea buys back his wife at the slave market price — fifteen shekels, half the standard thirty — the covenant portrait of YHWH's redemption of the covenant community from the slavery of covenant apostasy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 4 — the children of Yisra'EL shall dwell many days without king or prince, without sacrifice or pillar, without ephod or household gods. The covenant state of the exile is precisely described: stripped of both legitimate covenant structures — sacrifice, ephod — and illegitimate ones — pillar, household gods. The exile is covenant emptying before covenant refilling.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 5 — afterward they shall return and seek YHWH their Creator, and Dawid their king. The covenant messianic promise in the context of the northern kingdom exile: the restoration is not merely political return to the land but covenant return to YHWH and to the Davidic covenant — the unified covenant monarchy that the northern kingdom rejected at the division.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — I bought her for fifteen shekels of silver and a homer and a lethech of barley. The covenant purchase price is deliberately below the standard slave redemption price of thirty shekels — Shemot 21:32. The degraded price reflects the covenant condition of the covenant community: not at full covenant value, but redeemed nonetheless. YHWH does not wait for the covenant community to restore its covenant value before redeeming it — he redeems it in its diminished state.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 4 Hear the Word of YHWH — No Faithfulness or Steadfast Love — My People Are Destroyed for Lack of Knowledge — The Priests Have Rejected Knowledge — Like People Like Priest

1 Hear the word of YHWH, O children of Yisra'EL, for YHWH has a controversy with the inhabitants of the land. There is no faithfulness or steadfast love, and no knowledge of Creator in the land.

2 There is swearing, lying, murder, stealing, and committing adultery. They break all bounds, and bloodshed follows bloodshed.

3 Therefore the land mourns, and all who dwell in it languish, and also the beasts of the field and the birds of the heavens, and even the fish of the sea are taken away.

4 Yet let no one contend, and let none accuse, for with you is my contention, O priest.

5 You shall stumble by day. The prophet also shall stumble with you by night. And I will destroy your mother.

6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge. Because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the Torah of your Creator, I also will forget your children.

7 The more they increased, the more they sinned against me. I will change their glory into shame.

8 They feed on the sin of my people. They are greedy for their iniquity.

9 And it shall be like people, like priest. I will punish them for their ways and repay them for their deeds.

10 They shall eat, but not be satisfied. They shall play the whore, but not multiply, because they have forsaken YHWH to cherish whoredom.

11 Whoredom, wine, and new wine, which take away the understanding.

12 My people inquire of a piece of wood, and their walking staff gives them oracles. For a spirit of whoredom has led them astray, and they have left their Creator to play the whore.

13 They sacrifice on the tops of the mountains and burn offerings on the hills, under oak, poplar, and terebinth, because their shade is good. Therefore your daughters play the whore, and your brides commit adultery.

14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore, nor your brides when they commit adultery. For the men themselves go aside with prostitutes and sacrifice with cult prostitutes, and a people without understanding shall come to ruin.

15 Though you play the whore, O Yisra'EL, let not Yehudah become guilty. Enter not into Gil'gal, nor go up to Beit-Aven, and swear not, As YHWH lives.

16 Like a stubborn heifer, Yisra'EL is stubborn. Can YHWH now feed them like a lamb in a broad pasture?

17 Efrayim is joined to idols. Leave him alone.

18 When their drink is gone, they give themselves to whoring. Their rulers dearly love shame.

19 A wind has wrapped them in its wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 4 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 4 is the covenant lawsuit — riv — of YHWH against the land. The absence of three covenant foundations — faithfulness, steadfast love, knowledge of Creator — is the covenant diagnosis of the covenant community's condition. Verse 6 — my people are destroyed for lack of knowledge — is the covenant indictment that has defined the book's theological impact: covenant ignorance is covenant death.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 6 — because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the Torah of your Creator, I also will forget your children. The covenant reciprocity of rejection: the priests rejected the covenant knowledge they were charged to transmit — YHWH rejects them from the covenant office that required covenant knowledge.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. Verse 9 — like people, like priest. The covenant leveling of religious and social leadership: when the covenant leadership fails, the covenant community fails with it, and both receive the same covenant judgment. The covenant priest cannot claim covenant immunity from the covenant consequences he has helped produce.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-2 — no faithfulness, no steadfast love, no knowledge of Creator in the land — swearing, lying, murder, stealing, adultery — bloodshed follows bloodshed. The covenant catalog of absence and presence: the three covenant virtues are absent; the five covenant violations are present and compounding. The covenant community has the wrong things and lacks the right things simultaneously. This is the covenant diagnosis of total covenant inversion.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 5 Hear This, O Priests — A Spirit of Whoredom — They Shall Go With Their Flocks to Seek YHWH — I Will Be Like a Lion to Efrayim

1 Hear this, O priests. Pay attention, O house of Yisra'EL. Give ear, O house of the king. For the judgment is for you, for you have been a snare at Mitzpah and a net spread upon Tabor.

2 And the revolters have gone deep into slaughter, but I will discipline all of them.

3 I know Efrayim, and Yisra'EL is not hidden from me. For now, O Efrayim, you have played the whore. Yisra'EL is defiled.

4 Their deeds do not permit them to return to their Creator. For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they know not YHWH.

5 The pride of Yisra'EL testifies to his face. Yisra'EL and Efrayim shall stumble in their guilt. Yehudah also shall stumble with them.

6 With their flocks and herds they shall go to seek YHWH, but they will not find him. He has withdrawn from them.

7 They have dealt faithlessly with YHWH. For they have borne alien children. Now the new moon shall devour them with their fields.

8 Blow the horn in Giv'ah, the trumpet in Ramah. Sound the alarm at Beit-Aven. Tremble, O Binyamin.

9 Efrayim shall become a desolation in the day of punishment. Among the tribes of Yisra'EL I make known what is sure.

10 The princes of Yehudah have become like those who move the landmark. Upon them I will pour out my wrath like water.

11 Efrayim is oppressed, crushed in judgment, because he was determined to go after filth.

12 But I am like a moth to Efrayim, and like dry rot to the house of Yehudah.

13 When Efrayim saw his sickness, and Yehudah saw his wound, then Efrayim went to Assyria, and sent to the great king. But he is not able to cure you or heal your wound.

14 For I will be like a lion to Efrayim, and like a young lion to the house of Yehudah. I, even I, will tear and go away. I will carry off, and no one shall rescue.

15 I will return again to my place, until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face, and in their distress earnestly seek me.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 5 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 15 — I will return again to my place, until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face. The covenant withdrawal theology: YHWH's covenant presence withdraws until the covenant community's distress drives them to genuine covenant seeking. The covenant absence is not permanent abandonment but covenant discipline designed to produce covenant acknowledgment.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 6 — with their flocks and herds they shall go to seek YHWH, but they will not find him. He has withdrawn from them. The covenant warning against performative covenant seeking: coming to YHWH with the external apparatus of covenant worship — flocks and herds — without the covenant internal reorientation that genuine covenant seeking requires produces only the experience of covenant absence.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 5. Verse 13 — Efrayim went to Assyria, and sent to the great king. But he is not able to cure you or heal your wound. The political analysis embedded in the covenant oracle: Yisra'EL's response to covenant illness is political alliance with Assyria — seeking military and political healing for a covenant wound. The covenant wound cannot be healed by the military instrument that will eventually destroy the covenant patient.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — their deeds do not permit them to return to their Creator. For the spirit of whoredom is within them. The covenant bondage of covenant habit: the covenant community's accumulated covenant violations have produced a covenant internal condition — a spirit of whoredom — that disables the very mechanism of covenant return. Covenant sin produces a covenant incapacity for covenant repentance. The solution requires the covenant internal transformation promised in Yirmeyahu 31 and Yehezkel 36.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 6 Come Let Us Return to YHWH — After Two Days He Will Revive Us — What Shall I Do With You, O Efrayim — Your Love Is Like a Morning Cloud — I Desire Steadfast Love and Not Sacrifice

1 Come, let us return to YHWH. For he has torn us, that he may heal us. He has struck us down, and he will bind us up.

2 After two days he will revive us. On the third day he will raise us up, that we may live before him.

3 Let us know. Let us press on to know YHWH. His going out is sure as the dawn. He will come to us as the showers, as the spring rains that water the earth.

4 What shall I do with you, O Efrayim? What shall I do with you, O Yehudah? Your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes early away.

5 Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets. I have slain them by the words of my mouth. And my judgment goes forth as the light.

6 For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of Creator rather than burnt offerings.

7 But like Adam they transgressed the covenant. There they dealt faithlessly with me.

8 Gil'ad is a city of evildoers, tracked with blood.

9 As robbers lie in wait for a man, so the priests band together. They murder on the way to Shekhem. They commit villainy.

10 In the house of Yisra'EL I have seen a horrible thing. Efrayim's whoredom is there. Yisra'EL is defiled.

11 For you also, O Yehudah, a harvest is appointed. When I restore the fortunes of my people,

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 6 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 6 — I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of Creator rather than burnt offerings — is the most cited covenant declaration in the book and the covenant hermeneutical key to the entire Hoshea corpus. The covenant hierarchy is explicit: hesed — steadfast covenant love — and knowledge of Creator are categorically superior to the covenant ritual system.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 7 — like Adam they transgressed the covenant. The covenant connection to the original covenant breach: Yisra'EL's covenant violation follows the same pattern as the first covenant breach in the garden — covenant privilege, covenant command, covenant transgression, covenant consequence. The covenant history of Yisra'EL recapitulates the covenant history of Adam.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 6. Verse 4 — your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes early away. YHWH's covenant lament over the covenant community's covenant ephemerality: the covenant love — hesed — that Yisra'EL and Yehudah display is genuine but transient. It evaporates before it can be sustained into covenant obedience.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-3 — the repentance speech of the covenant community. Come, let us return. After two days he will revive us. Let us press on to know YHWH. YHWH's response — verse 4 — is not affirmation but lamentation: your love is like a morning cloud. The covenant community's repentance speech is liturgically correct but covenantally shallow. The problem is not the absence of repentance language but the absence of the covenant depth that makes covenant repentance durable.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 7 Efrayim's Iniquity Is Uncovered — They Are All Adulterers — An Unturned Cake — Gray Hairs Are Sprinkled on Him — Efrayim Is a Silly Dove — Woe to Them

1 When I would heal Yisra'EL, the iniquity of Efrayim is revealed, and the evil deeds of Samaria, for they deal falsely. The thief breaks in, and the bandits raid outside.

2 But they do not consider that I remember all their evil. Now their deeds surround them. They are before my face.

3 By their evil they make the king glad, and the princes by their treachery.

4 They are all adulterers. They are like a heated oven whose baker ceases to stir the fire, from the kneading of the dough until it is leavened.

5 On the day of our king, the princes became sick with the heat of wine. He stretched out his hand with mockers.

6 For with hearts like an oven they approach their intrigue. Their anger smolders all night. In the morning it blazes like a flaming fire.

7 All of them are hot as an oven, and they devour their rulers. All their kings have fallen, and none of them calls upon me.

8 Efrayim mixes himself with the peoples. Efrayim is a cake not turned.

9 Strangers devour his strength, and he knows it not. Gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, and he knows it not.

10 The pride of Yisra'EL testifies to his face. Yet they do not return to YHWH their Creator, nor seek him, for all this.

11 Efrayim is like a silly dove, without sense. They call to Egypt, they go to Assyria.

12 As they go, I will spread over them my net. I will bring them down like birds of the heavens. I will discipline them according to the report made to their congregation.

13 Woe to them, for they have strayed from me. Destruction to them, for they have rebelled against me. I would redeem them, but they speak lies against me.

14 They do not cry to me from the heart, but they wail upon their beds. For grain and wine they gash themselves. They rebel against me.

15 Although I trained and strengthened their arms, yet they devise evil against me.

16 They return, but not upward. They are like a treacherous bow. Their princes shall fall by the sword because of the insolence of their tongue. This shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 7 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The oven metaphor — verses 4-7 — is the covenant image of smoldering political passion: the conspiracy that produced Yisra'EL's political instability — multiple assassinated kings within a generation — is the heated oven of unrestrained covenant political desire. All their kings have fallen — verse 7 — and none of them calls upon me.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 11 — Efrayim is like a silly dove, without sense. They call to Egypt, they go to Assyria. The covenant foreign policy diagnosis: the northern kingdom oscillates between the two great powers — Egypt and Assyria — seeking protection from each against the other. YHWH's covenant analysis is that this political shuttling is covenant stupidity: the dove without sense flying between two predators.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 7. Verse 8 — Efrayim is a cake not turned. The covenant bread metaphor: a cake baked on one side only is burned on the bottom and raw on top — useless. Efrayim's cultural mixing with the surrounding nations — mixing himself with the peoples — produces covenant incoherence: burned toward the world, raw toward YHWH.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13-14 — I would redeem them, but they speak lies against me. They do not cry to me from the heart. The covenant desire of YHWH expressed in covenant tension: YHWH wants to redeem — the covenant impulse toward covenant restoration is present — but the covenant community's response is covenant falsity and performative covenant lament. The prayer is there — wailing — but not the covenant heart engagement that transforms prayer into genuine covenant communication.

THE COVENANT LAWSUIT AND THE COVENANT COMPASSION — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 14

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 8 Set the Trumpet to Your Lips — They Have Transgressed My Covenant — They Have Made Kings But Not Through Me — Samaria's Calf — They Sow the Wind — Israel Is Swallowed Up

1 Set the trumpet to your lips. One like a vulture is over the house of YHWH, because they have transgressed my covenant and rebelled against my Torah.

2 To me they cry, My Creator, we Yisra'EL know you.

3 Yisra'EL has spurned the good. The enemy shall pursue him.

4 They made kings, but not through me. They set up princes, but I knew it not. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction.

5 I have spurned your calf, O Samaria. My anger burns against them. How long will they be incapable of innocence?

6 For it is from Yisra'EL. A craftsman made it. It is not Creator. The calf of Samaria shall be broken to pieces.

7 For they sow the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind. The standing grain has no heads. It shall yield no flour. If it were to yield, strangers would devour it.

8 Yisra'EL is swallowed up. Already they are among the nations as a useless vessel.

9 For they have gone up to Assyria, a wild donkey wandering alone. Efrayim has hired lovers.

10 Though they hire among the nations, I will soon gather them up. And the king and princes shall soon writhe because of the tribute.

11 Because Efrayim has multiplied altars for sinning, they have become to him altars for sinning.

12 Were I to write for him my Torah by ten thousands, they would be regarded as a strange thing.

13 As for my sacrificial offerings, they sacrifice meat and eat it, but YHWH does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins. They shall return to Egypt.

14 For Yisra'EL has forgotten his Maker and built palaces, and Yehudah has multiplied fortified cities. So I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour his strongholds.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 8 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 7 — they sow the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind — is the covenant agricultural metaphor that has become proverbial across cultures. The covenant principle of covenant consequence: the covenant investment determines the covenant harvest. The covenant community invested in covenant alternatives — political power, religious innovation, foreign alliance — and the covenant harvest is proportionally catastrophic.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 12 — were I to write for him my Torah by ten thousands, they would be regarded as a strange thing. The covenant indictment of covenant alienation: YHWH's own Torah, however abundantly provided, has become foreign to the covenant community. The covenant community is alienated from its own covenant inheritance.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 8. Verse 4 — they made kings, but not through me. They set up princes, but I knew it not. The covenant constitutional violation of the northern kingdom: the dynasty succession by assassination — Shallum, Menahem, Pekahyah, Pekah — bypassed covenant divine appointment. The covenant political system of the northern kingdom was covenant self-governance without covenant authorization.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — to me they cry, My Creator, we Yisra'EL know you. YHWH's citation of Yisra'EL's covenant claim: in the very moment of covenant judgment, the covenant community asserts covenant knowledge of YHWH. The covenant claim is immediately juxtaposed — verse 3 — with the covenant reality: Yisra'EL has spurned the good. The covenant profession and covenant practice are in complete covenant contradiction.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 9 Rejoice Not, O Yisra'EL — They Shall Return to Egypt — The Prophet Is a Fool — Efrayim's Glory Shall Fly Away — I Found Yisra'EL Like Grapes in the Wilderness

1 Rejoice not, O Yisra'EL. Exult not like the peoples. For you have played the whore, forsaking your Creator. You have loved a prostitute's wages on all threshing floors.

2 Threshing floor and wine vat shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail them.

3 They shall not remain in the land of YHWH, but Efrayim shall return to Egypt, and they shall eat unclean food in Assyria.

4 They shall not pour drink offerings of wine to YHWH, and their sacrifices shall not please him. It shall be like mourners' bread to them. All who eat of it shall be defiled, for their bread shall be for their hunger only. It shall not come to the house of YHWH.

5 What will you do on the day of the appointed feast, and on the day of the feast of YHWH?

6 For behold, they are going away from destruction. But Egypt shall gather them. Memphis shall bury them. Nettles shall possess their precious things of silver. Thorns shall be in their tents.

7 The days of punishment have come. The days of recompense have come. Yisra'EL shall know it. The prophet is a fool. The man of the spirit is mad, because of your great iniquity and great hatred.

8 The prophet is the watchman of Efrayim with my Creator, yet a fowler's snare is on all his ways, and hatred in the house of his Creator.

9 They have deeply corrupted themselves as in the days of Giv'ah. He will remember their iniquity. He will punish their sins.

10 Like grapes in the wilderness, I found Yisra'EL. Like the first fruit on the fig tree in its first season, I saw your fathers. But they came to Ba'al-Peor and consecrated themselves to the thing of shame, and became detestable like the thing they loved.

11 Efrayim's glory shall fly away like a bird — no birth, no pregnancy, no conception.

12 Even if they bring up children, I will bereave them till none is left. Woe to them when I depart from them.

13 Efrayim, as I have seen, was like Tzor, planted in a meadow. But Efrayim must lead his children out to slaughter.

14 Give them, O YHWH — what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.

15 Every evil of theirs is in Gilgal. There I began to hate them. Because of the wickedness of their deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no more. All their princes are rebels.

16 Efrayim is stricken. Their root is dried up. They shall bear no fruit. Even though they give birth, I will put their beloved children to death.

17 My Creator will reject them because they have not listened to him. They shall be wanderers among the nations.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 9 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The harvest celebration context of verse 1 — the threshing floor — is the covenant inversion of the Shavuot harvest setting: the covenant community cannot celebrate the covenant harvest because the covenant land will be stripped. The covenant festivals of the restored covenant will become the covenant mourning of the exile.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 10 — like grapes in the wilderness, I found Yisra'EL. The covenant nostalgia of YHWH: the early covenant relationship between YHWH and Yisra'EL — the wilderness period — was the covenant first fruit, delightful and unexpected. The covenant deterioration from that original covenant delight is the measure of the covenant tragedy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 9. Verse 7 — the prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit is mad, because of your great iniquity. The covenant social inversion: when the covenant community's iniquity is great enough, it experiences the true covenant prophet as insane and foolish. The covenant diagnostic of a community in deep covenant distress: it cannot distinguish the true covenant voice from madness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14 — Give them, O YHWH — what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts. Hoshea's covenant intercession is the most disturbing in the prophetic corpus: the prophet asks YHWH for reproductive failure for the covenant community. The covenant logic — verse 11-13 — is covenant mercy: it is better for the covenant children not to be born than to be born into slaughter. Covenant intercession here inverts conventional covenant expectations.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 10 Yisra'EL Is a Luxuriant Vine — Their Heart Is False — Sow for Yourselves Righteousness — You Have Plowed Iniquity — Seek YHWH

1 Yisra'EL is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit increased, the more altars he built. The better his land, the better the pillars he made.

2 Their heart is false. Now they must bear their guilt. YHWH will break down their altars and destroy their pillars.

3 For now they will say, We have no king, for we do not fear YHWH. And a king — what could he do for us?

4 They utter mere words. With empty oaths they make covenants. So judgment springs up like poisonous weeds in the furrows of the field.

5 The inhabitants of Samaria tremble for the calf of Beit-Aven. Its people mourn for it, and so do its idolatrous priests who rejoiced over it and over its glory. For it has departed from them.

6 The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Efrayim shall be put to shame, and Yisra'EL shall be ashamed of his idol.

7 Samaria's king shall perish like a twig on the face of the waters.

8 The high places of Aven, the sin of Yisra'EL, shall be destroyed. Thorn and thistle shall grow up on their altars, and they shall say to the mountains, Cover us, and to the hills, Fall on us.

9 From the days of Giv'ah you have sinned, O Yisra'EL. There they have continued. Shall not the war against the unjust overtake them in Giv'ah?

10 When I please, I will discipline them, and nations shall be gathered against them when they are bound up for their double iniquity.

11 Efrayim was a trained heifer that loved to thresh. And I spared her fair neck. But I will put Efrayim to the yoke. Yehudah must plow. Yaakov must harrow for himself.

12 Sow for yourselves righteousness. Reap steadfast love. Break up your fallow ground, for it is the time to seek YHWH, that he may come and rain righteousness upon you.

13 You have plowed iniquity. You have reaped injustice. You have eaten the fruit of lies, because you have trusted in your own way and in the multitude of your warriors.

14 Therefore the tumult of war shall arise among your people, and all your fortresses shall be destroyed, as Shalman destroyed Beit-Arvel on the day of battle. Mothers were dashed in pieces with their children.

15 Thus it shall be done to you, O Beit-El, because of your great evil. At dawn the king of Yisra'EL shall be utterly cut off.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 10 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 12 — sow for yourselves righteousness, reap steadfast love, break up your fallow ground, for it is the time to seek YHWH — is the covenant agricultural appeal for covenant renewal: the covenant ground of the covenant community has been left fallow — uncultivated covenant potential. The time for covenant cultivation is now, before the covenant judgment arrives.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 1 — the more his fruit increased, the more altars he built. The better his land, the better the pillars he made. The covenant inversion of covenant prosperity: instead of covenant prosperity producing covenant gratitude directed toward YHWH, it produced expansion of the competing covenant worship systems. Covenant abundance became the fuel for covenant apostasy.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 10. Verse 4 — they utter mere words. With empty oaths they make covenants. So judgment springs up like poisonous weeds. The covenant speech-act analysis: covenant words without covenant substance — political covenants made with empty oaths — produce covenant jurisprudential toxicity. The language of covenant is deployed without covenant intention, poisoning the covenant social environment.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11-13 — Efrayim was a trained heifer that loved to thresh — the pleasant agricultural work. Now the yoke awaits. You have plowed iniquity, you have reaped injustice. The covenant agricultural sequence: the kind of covenant plowing determines the kind of covenant harvest. Iniquity-plowing produces injustice-harvest. Righteousness-sowing produces steadfast-love reaping — verse 12. The covenant agricultural metaphor makes the covenant consequence structure inescapable: the harvest is always the crop that was planted.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 11 When Yisra'EL Was a Child I Loved Him — I Taught Efrayim to Walk — They Did Not Know That I Healed Them — My Heart Recoils Within Me — I Will Not Execute My Burning Anger

1 When Yisra'EL was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.

2 The more they were called, the more they went away. They kept sacrificing to the Ba'alim and burning offerings to idols.

3 Yet it was I who taught Efrayim to walk. I took them up by their arms, but they did not know that I healed them.

4 I led them with cords of kindness, with the bands of love, and I became to them as one who eases the yoke on their jaws, and I bent down to them and fed them.

5 They shall not return to the land of Egypt, but Assyria shall be their king, because they have refused to return to me.

6 The sword shall rage against their cities, consume the bars of their gates, and devour them because of their own counsels.

7 My people are bent on turning away from me, and though they call out to the Most High, he shall not raise them up at all.

8 How can I give you up, O Efrayim? How can I hand you over, O Yisra'EL? How can I make you like Admah? How can I treat you like Tzevo'im? My heart recoils within me. My compassion grows warm and tender.

9 I will not execute my burning anger. I will not again destroy Efrayim, for I am Creator and not a man, the Holy One in your midst, and I will not come in wrath.

10 They shall go after YHWH. He will roar like a lion. When he roars, his children shall come trembling from the west.

11 They shall come trembling like birds from Egypt, and like doves from the land of Assyria, and I will return them to their homes, declares YHWH.

12 Efrayim has surrounded me with lies, and the house of Yisra'EL with deceit. But Yehudah still walks with Creator and is faithful to the Holy One.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 11 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 11 is the most intimate covenant passage in Hoshea — YHWH speaks as the covenant father, not the covenant husband. The covenant father imagery — teaching to walk, holding by the arms, feeding, leading with bands of love — is the covenant domestic portrait of YHWH's early relationship with Yisra'EL as a covenant child.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 8 — how can I give you up, O Efrayim? My heart recoils within me. My compassion grows warm and tender. The covenant self-interrogation of YHWH: the covenant Father cannot execute the full covenant judgment because the covenant love is deeper than the covenant anger. The covenant compassion overrides the covenant justice — not by denying it but by transcending it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 11. Verse 9 — I am Creator and not a man, the Holy One in your midst, and I will not come in wrath. The covenant ontological declaration: YHWH's covenant mercy exceeds what any human would show, precisely because YHWH is not human. The covenant restraint of wrath is not weakness but the expression of covenant divine transcendence over the covenant judicial impulse.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — when Yisra'EL was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son. The covenant childhood of the covenant people: the Exodus is re-read as the covenant Father calling the covenant child out of Egypt. The covenant love precedes and motivates the covenant rescue — YHWH did not love Yisra'EL because of the Exodus; the Exodus happened because of the covenant love. Verse 2 — the more they were called, the more they went away. The covenant vocation to covenant freedom produced covenant flight from the covenant Caller.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 12 Efrayim Feeds on the Wind — He Strove With the Angel — Yaakov Fled to Aram — By a Prophet YHWH Brought Yisra'EL From Egypt — Efrayim Has Bitterly Provoked

1 Efrayim feeds on the wind and pursues the east wind all day long. They multiply falsehood and violence. They make a covenant with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.

2 YHWH has an indictment against Yehudah and will punish Yaakov according to his ways. He will repay him according to his deeds.

3 In the womb he took his brother by the heel, and in his manhood he strove with Creator.

4 He strove with the angel and prevailed. He wept and sought his favor. He met Creator at Beit-El, and there Creator spoke with us —

5 YHWH, the Creator of hosts, YHWH is his memorial name.

6 So you, by the help of your Creator, return. Hold fast to love and justice, and wait continually for your Creator.

7 A merchant, in whose hands are false balances, he loves to oppress.

8 Efrayim has said, Ah, but I am rich. I have found wealth for myself. In all my labors they cannot find in me iniquity or sin.

9 I am YHWH your Creator from the land of Egypt. I will again make you dwell in tents, as in the days of the appointed feast.

10 I spoke to the prophets. It was I who multiplied visions, and through the prophets gave parables.

11 If there is iniquity in Gil'ad, they shall surely come to nothing. In Gilgal they sacrifice bulls. Their altars also are like stone heaps on the furrows of the field.

12 Yaakov fled to the land of Aram. There Yisra'EL served for a wife, and for a wife he guarded sheep.

13 By a prophet YHWH brought Yisra'EL up from Egypt, and by a prophet he was guarded.

14 Efrayim has bitterly provoked him to anger. So his Creator will leave his bloodguilt on him and will repay him for his disgraceful deeds.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 12 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The Yaakov typology of Chapter 12 connects the covenant community's current covenant struggle to the covenant patriarch's founding covenant struggles: in the womb he grasped the heel, he strove with Creator and prevailed. The covenant invitation — verse 6 — is to return to the same covenant mode as Yaakov: hold fast to love and justice, wait for your Creator.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 4 — he strove with the angel and prevailed. He wept and sought his favor. The Orit tradition preserves the covenant wrestling at the Yabok ford as a covenant model of persistent covenant engagement: Yaakov did not merely resist but wept and sought covenant favor. The covenant struggle produces covenant blessing only when it includes covenant humility.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 12. Verse 8 — Efrayim has said, I am rich. I have found wealth for myself. In all my labors they cannot find in me iniquity or sin. The covenant self-exculpation of covenant prosperity: the northern kingdom interprets its economic success as covenant innocence. The covenant error is the equation of material blessing with covenant moral status — a covenant category error that Hoshea consistently dismantles.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — by a prophet YHWH brought Yisra'EL up from Egypt, and by a prophet he was guarded. The covenant elevation of the prophetic office in the covenant historical retrospective: Mosheh — the archetypal covenant prophet — was the covenant instrument of the foundational covenant event. The covenant prophetic tradition is not a later addition to the covenant structure but the means by which YHWH's covenant purposes are executed from the beginning.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 13 When Efrayim Spoke There Was Trembling — Like the Morning Mist — O Yisra'EL You Are Against Me Your Helper — Where Is Your King — I Will Ransom Them From the Power of Sheol

1 When Efrayim spoke, there was trembling. He was exalted in Yisra'EL, but he incurred guilt through Ba'al and died.

2 And now they sin more and more, and make for themselves metal images, idols skillfully made of their silver, all of them the work of craftsmen. It is said of them, Those who offer human sacrifice kiss calves.

3 Therefore they shall be like the morning mist or like the dew that goes early away, like the chaff that swirls from the threshing floor or like smoke from a window.

4 But I am YHWH your Creator from the land of Egypt, and you know no Creator but me, and besides me there is no savior.

5 It was I who knew you in the wilderness, in the land of drought.

6 But when they had grazed, they became full. They were filled, and their heart was lifted up. Therefore they forgot me.

7 So I am to them like a lion. Like a leopard I will lurk beside the way.

8 I will fall upon them like a bear robbed of her cubs. I will tear open their breast, and there I will devour them like a lion, as a wild beast would rip them open.

9 He destroys you, O Yisra'EL, for you are against me, against your helper.

10 Where now is your king, to save you in all your cities? Where are all your rulers — those of whom you said, Give me a king and princes?

11 I gave you a king in my anger, and I took him away in my wrath.

12 The iniquity of Efrayim is bound up. His sin is kept in store.

13 The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son, for at the right time he does not present himself at the opening of the womb.

14 I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol. I shall redeem them from Death. O Death, where are your plagues? O Sheol, where is your sting? Compassion is hidden from my eyes.

15 Though he may flourish among his brothers, the east wind, the wind of YHWH, shall come, rising from the wilderness, and his fountain shall dry up. His spring shall be parched. It shall strip his treasury of every precious vessel.

16 Samaria shall bear her guilt, because she has rebelled against her Creator. They shall fall by the sword. Their little ones shall be dashed in pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 13 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Verse 14 — I shall ransom them from the power of Sheol. I shall redeem them from Death. O Death, where are your plagues? O Sheol, where is your sting? The covenant declaration of YHWH's sovereignty over death — the ultimate covenant reversal. The same verse that pronounces covenant judgment in context — compassion is hidden — contains the covenant language of death's defeat.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 4-6 — I am YHWH your Creator from the land of Egypt, and you know no Creator but me. It was I who knew you in the wilderness. But when they had grazed, they became full — their heart was lifted up — therefore they forgot me. The covenant cycle of covenant need, covenant rescue, covenant prosperity, covenant forgetting is here compressed to its essential elements.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 13. Verse 11 — I gave you a king in my anger, and I took him away in my wrath. The covenant history of the northern kingdom monarchy compressed into one verse: YHWH gave Yisra'EL the kingdom they demanded in covenant anger — as he gave them quail in the wilderness — and removes it in the same covenant anger. The covenant gift of an unauthorized covenant institution carries the seeds of its own covenant termination.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — when they had grazed, they became full. They were filled, and their heart was lifted up. Therefore they forgot me. The covenant anatomy of covenant prosperity's spiritual danger: the sequence is need — satisfaction — fullness — pride — forgetting YHWH. Covenant satiety produces covenant amnesia. The covenant wilderness period — verse 5 — was the period of maximum covenant knowledge because it was the period of maximum covenant dependency. Covenant sufficiency produces covenant spiritual blindness.

HOSHEA — CHAPTER 14 Return O Yisra'EL to YHWH Your Creator — Take With You Words — I Will Heal Their Apostasy — I Will Be Like the Dew to Yisra'EL — Who Is Wise Let Him Understand

1 Return, O Yisra'EL, to YHWH your Creator, for you have stumbled because of your iniquity.

2 Take with you words and return to YHWH. Say to him, Take away all iniquity. Accept what is good, and we will pay with bulls the offerings of our lips.

3 Assyria shall not save us. We will not ride on horses. And we will say no more, Our Creator, to the work of our hands. In you the orphan finds mercy.

4 I will heal their apostasy. I will love them freely, for my anger has turned from them.

5 I will be like the dew to Yisra'EL. He shall blossom like the lily. He shall take root like the trees of Lebanon.

6 His shoots shall spread out. His beauty shall be like the olive, and his fragrance like Lebanon.

7 They shall return and dwell beneath my shadow. They shall flourish like the grain. They shall blossom like the vine. Their fame shall be like the wine of Lebanon.

8 O Efrayim, what have I to do with idols? It is I who answer and look after you. I am like an evergreen cypress. From me comes your fruit.

9 Whoever is wise, let him understand these things. Whoever is discerning, let him know them. For the ways of YHWH are right, and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 14 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The closing covenant invitation of Hoshea — Return, O Yisra'EL — is the covenant conclusion of the entire book: after 13 chapters of covenant accusation, covenant judgment, and covenant lament, the final chapter is the covenant call to covenant return and the covenant promise of covenant healing. Verse 4 — I will heal their apostasy — is the covenant resolution of the covenant illness diagnosed throughout the book.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 14 in full. Verse 2 — take with you words and return to YHWH. Say to him, Take away all iniquity. Accept what is good, and we will pay with bulls the offerings of our lips. The covenant liturgical provision: YHWH provides the covenant community with the covenant words for their covenant return. The covenant text itself becomes the covenant prayer. The offering of the lips replaces the animal offering as the covenant acceptable sacrifice.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 14. Verse 5-7 — I will be like the dew to Yisra'EL. He shall blossom like the lily. He shall take root like Lebanon. His beauty shall be like the olive and his fragrance like Lebanon. The covenant restoration imagery is botanical: the covenant community restored to covenant relationship with YHWH becomes a covenant garden of covenant fertility and covenant fragrance.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — whoever is wise, let him understand these things. Whoever is discerning, let him know them. For the ways of YHWH are right, and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them. The covenant wisdom coda of the book: the closing verses frame the entire Hoshea corpus as covenant wisdom literature. The covenant narrative of Chapter 1-13 is covenant instruction in the ways of YHWH. The covenant discerning reader who receives the instruction walks rightly; the covenant transgressor stumbles over the same covenant truth.

END OF PART ONE — HOSHEA

Volume Twenty — The Twelve Minor Prophets — Part One

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

CONTINUES IN PART TWO — YOEL AND AMOS
